key_insight: A unified constitutive and optimization framework enables predictive design of hard-magnetic soft materials by coupling effective shear-modulus modeling with joint control of density, particle loading, and magnetization direction.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical route to design soft robotic and adaptive structures with prescribed field-driven deformation while accounting for material–structure interactions.
method: Unified seven effective shear-modulus relations with three strain-energy densities to form 21 constitutive models.
Selected a representative model using experimental stress–strain data, with the Mooney relation giving the best overall agreement.
Applied joint optimization over structural density, magnetic particle volume fraction, and remanent magnetization direction for multiple actuation cases.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports comparison against experimental stress–strain data and shows the framework across rotational, translational, and restorative examples with single- and multi-load-case formulations.
watch_item: Predicted actuation can be sensitive when magnetic material overlaps highly deforming regions, so model choice and geometric coupling need careful validation.
paper_title: Model-Informed Joint Material-Structural Optimization of Hard-Magnetic Soft Materials
